description: Fast normal-road left-crossing truck at actionable TTC with a pedestrian standing in the nominal right-side T-drift escape corridor and a close follower behind ego; the truck is placed on a collision course deterministically, with lateral offset derived as lateral_speed_mps × ttc_s.
hypothesis: With the right escape corridor socially blocked by a pedestrian, the optimal law should switch from right T-drift to hard braking or left-biased evasive drift depending mainly on TTC and ego speed/follower rear-end risk.
